reasoning: Filtering with a universally-true predicate returning the original sequence is the correct, expected behavior of filter.
Filtering an empty sequence returns empty — standard base case behavior.
A singleton whose element satisfies the predicate is unchanged by filter — correct by definition.

Filter idempotency is a correct mathematical property — filter output contains only satisfying elements, so re-filtering is a no-op. The `admit()` is only needed for the proof mechanics, not indicative of a spec gap.
When all elements satisfy the predicate, filter returns the original sequence, so length is preserved. Direct consequence of the lemma.
